title: Madhuca lamii
intro: Madhuca lamii, also known as the Himalayan mahua, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the Sapotaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Madhuca lamii include triterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and to promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Madhuca lamii is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae. It belongs to the genus Madhuca. Madhuca lamii is a flowering plant native to India and Sri Lanka. The species is a member of the Sapotaceae family, which is a group of tropical evergreen trees and shrubs. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary history and its relationships with other species in the same family.
